metadata.title: Run the Three-Check Deployment Verification Protocol with Claude Code

beats.narration: The simulator works on your laptop. The three-check protocol finds the font that won't load on the school network before thirty students hit a blank screen.

Three checks catch three different failure modes. Functional checks what runs. Environment checks what the school network allows. Pedagogical checks whether a ninth-grader can actually use it without reading the documentation.
Ask Claude to generate the three-check deployment verification protocol for the sorting simulator.

props.command:
claude "Generate deployment verification for sorting simulator:
  (1) Functional: 5 browser-console tests,
    375px and 1024px rendering, all 3 algorithms
  (2) Environment: list external resource deps,
    network-sensitive elements, CORS requests
  (3) Pedagogical: 5 questions a ninth-grader
    should answer after 10 min unsupervised"

beats.narration: The protocol has three sections. Functional: five console tests. Environment: external resource audit — Google Fonts CDN flagged. Pedagogical: five questions answerable from on-screen annotations.

props.code:
# Deployment Verification Protocol

## Functional Check
1. console.log(errors) → expect []
2. window.innerWidth=375 → layout intact
3. bubbleSort([5,3,1]) → swap_count correct
4. mergeSort([5,3,1]) → comparison_count correct
5. quickSort([5,3,1]) → pivot displayed

## Environment Check
- fonts.googleapis.com ← school may block CDN
- No external API calls detected
- All assets inline ← PASS

## Pedagogical Check
1. Which sort made fewer comparisons?
2. What does a swap mean visually?

beats.narration: Functional check: all five pass. Environment check: one failure — Google Fonts CDN blocked on the school network. The font falls back to system-serif, which is readable but breaks the design. Fix: inline the font or use a system font stack. Pedagogical check: five questions answerable from the on-screen annotations without reading source code.
Add a fourth accessibility check using axe-core in browser devtools.

props.command:
claude "Add fourth check: accessibility.
  Run axe-core in browser devtools.
  What does it catch?
  Fix any critical issues."

beats.narration: Axe-core catches one critical issue: the step button has no aria-label — screen readers announce it as button with no context. Fix: add aria-label equal to Step forward one comparison. One line, added before deployment. The fourth check catches what the other three are blind to.

The three-check protocol is fifteen minutes that prevents a forty-minute class where the demo doesn't work. Functional, environment, pedagogical — each catches a category of failure the others miss. Add accessibility as the fourth when the school has screen-reader users.
Next: write the post-build document that makes your workflow trustworthy.
Like and subscribe for more Claude Code for Teachers.
